Cape Coral_FL.title: How does consumer confidence impact real estate? Lawrence ...
Cape Coral_FL.transcript: Consumer confidence, consumer sentiment currently is running very, very low. In fact, what is surprising to me is that the confidence level is below or almost matching what it had been 15 years ago when we had the Great Recession Massive Job Losses and foreclosure crisis. Today, the job market is soft, but we are clearly not in a recession. We don't have foreclosure but the confidence sentiment among consumers remaining very weak. We know home buying is a major decision, so without that confidence, it could delay or postpone some of that home purchase decisions. Now, the mortgage rate I have found that has a bigger influence can counter the confidence factor. So if somehow the mortgage rate was to retreat down towards three-year low, four-year low condition, maybe even with a weaker confidence, we still have some decent home buying activity.
Cape Coral_FL.script: Consumer confidence right now is at levels we haven't seen since the Great Recession. And that's wild because we don't have mass layoffs. We don't have foreclosures piling up. The job market is soft but people still have jobs. So why does this matter to you as a Cape Coral buyer? Because low confidence makes people hesitate, and hesitation in this market creates opportunity. Here's the thing though: mortgage rates have historically been a stronger trigger than confidence. When rates dropped to three and four year lows in past cycles, buyers came back even when the headlines were scary. Cape Coral inventory is sitting right now, sellers are negotiating, and if rates tick down even slightly this summer, that window closes fast. The buyers who moved during low confidence periods are the ones who got the best deals. Are you planning to buy in Cape Coral this year or waiting? Cape Coral_FL.transcript: with your seller or buyer, you always want to be planting the seeds that pricing is subjective. Pricing is subjective to everyone. The beauty is in the eye of the beholder. The person who likes it the most is willing to pay the most. If two parties are interested, which you don't find yourself in that situation on every listing, two parties are interested. Cape Coral_FL.script: Here is something most buyers in Georgia never hear until it is too late. If you are in a multiple offer situation, the house is not going to the highest bidder. It is going to the person who wants it the most. That is it. Pricing is subjective. Two people look at the same home in Savannah, in Atlanta, in Augusta, anywhere in Georgia, and they see completely different things. The one who falls in love with it pays the most. Simple. Where agents mess this up is by overcomplicating it. Escalation clauses, waived contingencies, cover letters. None of that matters if your number does not reflect how badly you want it. Cape Coral_FL.caption:
A lot of buyers and sellers ask me what an off-market property actually means — and the simplest answer is this:

It’s a property the seller has chosen not to put on the MLS.

The MLS is what feeds all the major real estate websites like Zillow, Redfin, Realtor.com, and Trulia. So when a property is kept off-market, it’s not being publicly advertised to the general public.

Instead, it’s typically shared more privately through:
✔️ Agent networks
✔️ Buyer databases
✔️ Investors
✔️ Private connections and outreach

Sometimes sellers want more privacy. Sometimes they want to test the waters. And sometimes buyers are looking for opportunities that never actually hit the open market.

The important thing to know is: just because a home isn’t listed publicly… doesn’t mean it’s not available.

If you’re curious about buying or selling off-market, I’d be happy to walk you through how it works.

But I don't want to guess what matters to you. Maybe you want to know if your street is in a high-risk flood zone. Maybe you want to know which zip codes are still under $400K. Maybe you want to know if now is even a good time to buy. Whatever it is, I want to answer it. So tell me this: what's the one thing about Cape Coral real estate you've always wanted to know but never got a straight answer on? Drop it in the comments and I'll make that video this week.

Because last month alone I helped a buyer catch a $14,000 inspection credit the seller didn't want to give, renegotiated after a low appraisal on a home in North Naples, and talked another client out of a contract that had a flood zone issue nobody flagged. None of that shows up on Instagram. You only hear about it when something goes wrong and nobody was there to catch it. The difference between a good agent and a bad one in Naples right now is easily $20,000 to $40,000 on a typical transaction. So tell me honestly, have you ever had a deal go sideways, or do you know someone who did? Drop it in the comments. I want to hear the real stories.

Now imagine what it would feel like if YOU didn't have to do all of this yourself…
And this is not about doing less…

It's about building a business that serves your Clients at the HIGHEST level… even when you're not the one doing every single thing 💜

1️⃣ ✨ Approve showings and chase feedback myself…
My incredible Admin sends every Buyer's agent a text… with me included… sharing access instructions, parking details, and everything they need to show beautifully. Within 24 hours she follows up for feedback so we are NEVER left guessing.

2️⃣ 📋 Create marketing materials from scratch…
Because of my S Y S T E M S… every listing has an extensive Marketing Plan ready to go. Yes, some pieces are templated… but my Marketing Team makes sure every home shines in its absolute best light 🤩
I still review everything before it goes live… always.

3️⃣ 🏡 Scramble to prepare for Open Houses…
I have an Open House Box always ready. SOPs, a workflow for potential Buyers, and my Admin creates beautiful flyers… not just for me but for the amazing agents who generously host 🙌🏼

4️⃣ 📧 Send transactional emails myself…
While I stay looped in on every transaction… my Transaction Manager is truly invaluable. She handles the details so I can focus on the conversations that matter most.
Thank you, Paige 👏🏼

5️⃣ 🎉 Scramble to remember past Client anniversaries…
My Admin reminds me every single month who has a milestone coming up… and yes, it all lives on my Google Calendar so nothing ever slips through the cracks.

6️⃣ 🔥 Reinvent the wheel for every transaction…
My step-by-step Checklists and SOPs mean every Buyer, Seller, and Military Family receives the same smooth, thoughtful experience every single time.

Their journeys are unique… but they can ALWAYS expect a certain level of hospitality and organization from our team ✨ 

💜 The goal is to protect your headspace so you can show up FULLY for your Clients… and for your Family.

Cape Coral_FL.transcript: For every day, it feels like vacation.

Cape Coral_FL.caption:
I’ve walked through hundreds of homes. And I can usually tell within the first five minutes if the owners kept up with it or let it slide.

Most buyers completely miss these. But they’ll cost you big after closing.

1. Black gunk around the base of the toilet Nobody wants to talk about this one. But if there’s discoloration or soft flooring around the base, that wax seal has been leaking. Water seeping into the subfloor for months. Maybe years. A $10
wax ring ignored long enough becomes a $2,000-$4,000 subfloor repair.

2. Grimy caulking around tubs and showers Black or peeling caulk isn’t just ugly. It means water has been getting behind the tile and into the walls. Mold thrives in that environment.
And once it’s behind the wall, you’re looking at a tear-out, not a quick re-caulk.
Average bathroom mold remediation runs $1,500-
$3,500.

3. Dryer vents caked with lint Check the vent behind the dryer. If it’s packed with lint and hasn’t been cleaned in years, that’s a fire hazard. Clogged dryer vents cause nearly 3,000 house fires per year. It also tells you everything about how the homeowner treats the stuff they can’t see.

4. HVAC filters that look like carpet Pop open that return vent. If the filter is gray, matted, and looks like it hasn’t been changed since the Obama administration, that system has been working overtime. Reduced airflow. Higher energy bills.
And a unit that’s going to die years before it should.
Replacing an HVAC system runs $8,000-$15,000

5. Water stains on the ceiling painted over They’ll paint over it hoping you won’t notice. But if the texture doesn’t match or there’s a faint ring showing through, there was a leak. Was the source actually repaired?

That’s the question your inspector needs to answer. An untreated roof leak can cause $10,000+ in structural damage.

None of these are deal breakers alone.
But stack two or three together and you’re looking at a home where deferred maintenance is the norm. And deferred maintenance always catches up with the next owner.

That next owner doesn’t have to be you.

Save this for when you go check out a 🏠

Build a life that’s beautiful enough to step away…

Cape Coral_FL.title: How Do I Price My Home to Sell in Cape Coral and NOT Sit! Make the right decision for you!
Cape Coral_FL.transcript: How do I price my home to sell, not sit, in Cape Coral? I'm Tom F. Miller, your Florida realtor, and today we're diving into a proven strategy from swirl-estate.com. Don't forget the dash. To sell without sitting, you price based on today's most relevant comps, align with how buyers search online, and use an early response plan so you don't chase the market. That's the core of our strategy. Most sellers don't lose money because their home is bad. They lose leverage because the price doesn't match the current buyer pool. Pricing is the number one reason homes sit. Look at the data. In February 2026, the median sale price in Cape Coral was about $375,000 with 70 days on market. Well-priced homes move. Overpriced ones get ignored. Overpricing creates an invisible penalty. You risk extra months of carrying costs for insurance, taxes, and maintenance, and you end up with a much weaker final negotiation. Step one, start with a tight, honest comp set. In Cape Coral, value swings based on your proximity to bridges, the age of your roof, and whether you're waterfront or off water. Step two, price for how buyers search. Most shop in rounded brackets like 400,000. If you list at 401,000, you might miss the perfect buyer capped at 400. Step three, read the market's speed limit. In Lee County, buyers feel comfortable negotiating when prices look stretched. Price accurately upfront to create urgency, not desperation. Step four, use a first 14-day plan. If showings are light in the first two weeks, we adjust quickly before the listing becomes stale. Your best buyers are always the early ones. Step five, decide your style. Market matches for a predictable sale, while magnet pricing slightly under the comps creates urgency and multiple offers. We'll pick what fits your goals. Use our don't sit checklist. Are you getting saves? Are showings converting to offers? If you have traffic but no offers, the market is telling you the price is off. Local details matter like golf access and seaw wall age. Also, insurance-driven pricing is real in Southwest Florida. Your price must reflect the reality of roof age and wind mitigation. If you list high just to leave room, you miss the best buyers and end up negotiating twice. First with a price drop, then with the buyer. Be right early, not hopeful early. If you're looking to sell and buy home in Southwest Florida in the same move, pricing is about timing and certainty. We map out your net sheet so you can plan your next move confidently. For a pricing plan that fits your home, reach out to me at swflreal-estate.com. Don't forget the dash. Call or text 239-313929. Like, subscribe, and let's get your home sold.

Cape Coral_FL.transcript: Pretty much most of what we do is offm market. Um, we have a massive underwriting team. We call it the acquisitions team. And we're just looking for needle in a hay stack. So, we built that entire system. There's about 20,000 multifamily properties in Ohio. Of that, about 4,000 fit my buy box. We just hammer those 4,000 as much as we can direct to sellers. But, it's very hard to get a seller to sell without working with the broker. They just want that confidence booster that someone else is rubber stamping that the purchase price is good. They're not getting taken advantage of. So, we've even had deals where we've gone direct to seller. They go out and get a broker just to validate the price we already gave them and pay them the commission just to feel better about it, which I fully understand. A lot of times it's a mom and pop owner, out of state owner, someone this may be the biggest thing they ever sell. They just want to make sure they're not leaving money on the table. So, what a lot we do is direct to seller uh with some deals. What's more common is we work with brokers direct to sellers and we try to stay in front of the brokers and have a really good reputation and rapport so that when they get those deals they think of us first, they call us first and we try to lock it up before they even have to put the the offer in the ring memorandum and push it out to the marketplace.

Cape Coral_FL.caption: Cape Coral home buyers: Zillow/Realtor.com tax estimates can be misleading. They often reflect previous owner exemptions (like homestead). New taxes are based on sale price, not past assessments. Expect ~1.4% of purchase price. #CapeCoralRealEstate #HomeBuyingTips #PropertyTaxes #FloridaRealEstate
Cape Coral_FL.author: berrycessnacapecoralfloridarealestate
Cape Coral_FL.fullName: Berry Cessna | Cape Coral Florida Real Estate
Cape Coral_FL.title: Cape Coral Home Buying: Zillow Tax Numbers MISLEADING! #shorts
Cape Coral_FL.transcript: When it comes to buying a home in Cape Coral, the tax numbers you see on Zillow or Realtor.com may not accurately reflect your future tax obligations. Let's break it down. Many buyers rely on online listings to get an idea of property taxes, but these figures can be misleading. The amount displayed is often based on the previous owner's tax bill, [music] which may include exemptions like the Florida homestead exemption. Additionally, future tax amounts are determined by the sale price, not the previous year's assessment. In Cape Coral, property taxes typically amount to around 1.4% of the purchase price. To get a more accurate estimate, I recommend using the Lee County Tax Estimator, which you can find by clicking the link in the description below.

Cape Coral_FL.caption:
People hear “30 minutes” in St. Johns County and think it’s far.

But that only feels true depending on where you’re coming from.

For us — coming from Vermont — everything was 45 minutes away anyway.

Groceries.
Sports.
Dinner.
Anything.

So when people say things feel “far” here…
it actually feels closer.

And the biggest difference?

The drive.

You’re not doing it in the dark, cold, and cloudy days of the Northeast.

You’re driving in the sunshine.
Windows down.
Palm trees, water views, moss-covered oaks.

Along the coastline.
Along the St. Johns River.
Through roads that actually make the drive feel… enjoyable.

Same time.
Completely different experience.

So yeah — you might be 20–30 minutes from the beach, restaurants, or shopping…

But it doesn’t feel like a chore.

It feels like part of the lifestyle.

Cape Coral_FL.caption:
Most people don't lose money in real estate because of bad luck. They lose it because they're guessing. 🏘️💸

The market is not random. It leaves clues everywhere. Inventory levels, days on market, and price reductions all tell you who actually has the leverage.

When homes are selling fast, and inventory is tight, sellers control the conversation. Buyers need to move quickly and compete. When listings start sitting and price drops increase, the power quietly shifts the other way.

This is the difference between reacting and positioning. The right data tells you when to push, when to wait, and how to structure your offer or pricing strategy.

If you want to understand exactly where your market stands before you make a move, DM us to connect with a KW Core agent and get a breakdown specific to your area.

Fort Myers_FL.transcript: Have you heard the myth about 20% down? You actually don't need 20% down to purchase a house. In some cases, it's not even the best option.
Fort Myers_FL.script: Nobody told you this when you started saving for a house, but you do not need 20% down in Naples. In fact, for a lot of buyers here, putting 20% down is actually the wrong move. FHA loans let you get in with 3.5% down. Conventional loans can be as low as 3%. And some programs in Collier County are still offering down payment assistance that most buyers never even hear about. Meanwhile, people are sitting on the sidelines waiting until they hit that magic number, watching Naples median prices stay stubbornly above $600K and inventory disappear every spring. The buyers I worked with last month who put less down? They're building equity right now while you're still saving. So tell me, what's your current budget or how much you've saved, and I'll show you exactly what programs you actually qualify for in Naples.

Fort Myers_FL.transcript: If there's one, you had to give one tip, like one habit, a new tool, a tactic, a mindset that an agent can adopt this week to start to really grow their business this year. So anything that comes in mind to you that's sort of, you know, something that folks can take away and try to get started on this week. This isn't groundbreaking. Everybody's heard this before, but that's why it's OSO relevant. Your attitude shapes everything. We determine what happens to us. We are the market. We are the people who get to show up every single day and decide what hours we're going to put in, what passion we're going to adjust to, what possibilities we're going to dream about, what thing we're going to get excited to do, you know, and that's all a choice. And so I just think that if you're going to be a winner in any profession, especially in residential real estate, you got to have a great attitude. You got to be an optimist and you have to expect great things to happen and you have to align with people who are doing what you want to do.
Fort Myers_FL.script: The one mindset shift that separates agents who are struggling right now from agents who are closing deals every single month in Naples. Your attitude is your business plan. I know you've heard that before, but hear me out. In a market like Naples where inventory on Gulf-front properties is still under 90 days and median prices are holding above $650K, the agents winning right now are not the ones with the biggest ad budget. They're the ones who show up every single day expecting to win, putting in the hours, dreaming bigger than the market tells them to, and surrounding themselves with people already doing what they want to do. I've watched this play out firsthand in Port Royal, Pelican Bay, and Aqualane Shores. Attitude closes deals. So tell me honestly, what's one habit you're committing to this week to grow your business? Drop it in the comments and let's hold each other accountable.

They tell you real estate is a cutthroat, lonely business where you have to guard your secrets. We highly recommend ignoring them. 👯‍♀️
Meet Shonnie. To this day, neither of us can actually remember how we met in this business, but we instantly clicked.In an industry that constantly pushes you to compete with the agent sitting next to you, we realized we were infinitely stronger when we combined our brains.

We are both proudly part of the Wolf Pack at eXp Realty, and we decided that building a business is just better when you do it with your friends.

That’s why we are joining forces to build our own downline through co-sponsorship. We aren’t interested in being solo operators trying to beat each other to a closing table. We want to build a room where everyone is winning, sharing strategies, and having fun doing it!

This is a simple principle that I have stuck by for my entire career in real estate. 

The more conversations you have the better the odds are that you find more success in this business. 

That can be done from cold calling, door knocking, networking events, social media, and on and on. 

Just talk to more people on a daily basis. 

It’s a numbers game. 

Cast as wide of a net as possible. 

Hi, my name is Tim Smith with Living in Idaho at LPT Realty and I’d love to be your go-to Idaho Real Estate Dude. I have helped so many families relocate to different areas in Idaho. One thing I constantly work on in real estate is asking better questions.

Whether it’s a buyer consultation or a seller meeting, the goal is to truly understand a client’s wants and needs. A great reminder I heard recently was: “Be more interested than interesting.”

By listening more, asking thoughtful questions, and following up with detailed buyer and seller intake forms, I’m able to better serve my clients and guide them through the process with clarity and confidence.

Have questions about buying or selling a home? Let’s connect.

Fort Myers_FL.script: Most Naples buyers think they're protected because they have an agent. But here's what nobody tells you: not all representation is equal, and the difference can cost you tens of thousands of dollars on a single deal. I've seen buyers in Naples lose negotiating leverage, miss inspection red flags, and overpay on closing costs simply because their agent didn't know the local market well enough to push back. We're talking neighborhoods like Pelican Bay, Vineyards, and North Naples where comps shift block by block and one wrong move is a $40,000 mistake. You deserve someone who treats your money like it's their own, not someone going through the motions. So tell me this: what's the biggest worry you have about buying or selling in Naples right now? Drop it in the comments and I'll answer every single one personally.

Fort Myers_FL.caption:
Right before your Instagram breakthrough as a realtor, you’re going to have these three weird urges.

1️⃣ You’ll want to completely change everything.
If you’ve been building on Instagram, suddenly you’ll think this isn’t working and you need to start cold calling FSBOs because you saw one reel of an agent having wild success and now that feels like the only way to win.

2️⃣ You’ll want to quit.
Deals will fall apart and it will feel like everything you’ve been doing is in vain.
You’ll start questioning if this is even what God called you to do and convince yourself maybe you were only meant to stay home and this dream was a distraction.

3️⃣ You’ll start complaining.
You’ll catch yourself saying things like you hate real estate, other agents are the worst, buyers are liars.
And without realizing it, you’re speaking things that are tearing down your mindset.

All of this is normal.
Every single one of us has been there.

The difference between the ones who make it and the ones who don’t is not that they don’t have these thoughts.

They just don’t let those thoughts lead.

They push through or they choose to trust God in the process.

Pick your hard.

God never promised this would be easy.
He said in this world you will have trouble but take heart I have overcome the world.

This work matters but it’s also just a blip compared to eternity.

That perspective has helped me let things roll off my back and stay focused on what God has actually called me to do. 🤍

Fort Myers_FL.caption:
🏡 Weston Florida Real Estate Market 2026 | What Online Data Is NOT Telling You

Why does real estate data online feel so confusing right now?

If you are watching the Weston Florida real estate market, you have probably seen headlines saying prices are up… while others say the market is slowing down. Both can be true and here is why.

Different platforms report the market using different measurement methods. Some track closed sales, others track active listings, price reductions, or pending contracts. In a luxury market like Weston Florida, this creates mixed signals that can easily mislead homeowners.

As a Top Realtor in Weston Florida, I help sellers understand what the numbers actually mean so they can price, position, and launch strategically without leaving money on the table.

Fort Myers_FL.script:
Number 19 from Tracy Stein's list is the ability to master your thoughts. What goes into your mind comes out in your actions and your words. Being able to master your thoughts allows you to show up on a more positive and enthusiastic level in your conversations, your communication, and your presentation. Mastering your thoughts requires that you restrict what goes in so you can control what comes out.

Number 20 is the ability to create words to persuade and influence others. I thought about this one a lot when I first read it about two months ago. The ability to write words to persuade and influence others is something worth really studying because the words we choose have a direct impact on how we connect with people.

How intentional are you about what you allow into your mind and the words you choose when communicating with clients?
Fort Myers_FL.transcript: Number 19 from Tracy Stein, the ability to master your thoughts. Or I like to think that the ability to master and control your thoughts. What goes in here and what goes in here comes out in our actions and the words that we use. So being able to master our thoughts allows us to always present on a more positive or more enthusiastic level in terms of our conversation, communication and presentation. So mastering your thoughts requires that we restrict what goes in so we can control what comes out. Master your thoughts. Number 20 on his list, the ability to create words to persuade and influence others. And I thought about this one a lot when I read this the first time about two months ago. The ability to write words to persuade and influence others. The easiest way to learn to influence a person in a sales presentation, whether it be to a buyer or a seller is to ask a lot of questions. So therefore if you take and say to yourself, I'm going to learn the master and become a master of asking questions. Just take a piece of paper like I have in my hand, take a pen and start writing down what do you normally say to a buyer or seller in terms of a presentation to persuade them to buy or sell from you versus somebody else. Then look at those words that you wrote carefully and then turn that words sentence into a question because a question is much more persuasive when you engage another party in a conversation communication. So we can become much more persuasive by asking questions that we probably will ever be from just in communicating and telling people what we think. And the last thing that Tracy had on his list of 21 great ideas was the ability to ask for help because the truth is everybody needs somebody to assist them in the process of growth. Whether it be a great school teacher, a mid school teacher, a high school teacher, a college professor, whether it be mom and dad, whether it be a couple of good buddies, whether it be a mastermind group, accountability partners, role play partners, a great broker, a great manager, a great consultant to coach like myself. Everybody needs help but the hardest part is asking for it because as I said earlier, now you have to eliminate the ego from this whole conversation. So the ability to ask for help.

Fort Myers_FL.title: Think timing your sale does not matter? It can make a big ...
Fort Myers_FL.transcript: Think timing yourself doesn't matter, think again. The right timing could add thousands to your home sales price. Let me explain why. Many sellers don't realize this, but timing isn't just about the market. It's about understanding local trends and buyer psychology. North of Virginia's market has certain windows throughout the year when buyer activity is at its peak. These are the times when you can really maximize your sale price. I've seen so many sellers wait just a little too long to list, and it ended up costing them thousands. Buyers are looking for specific things during different seasons, and knowing when to list your home can make all the difference. It's not about waiting for a perfect moment. It's about knowing when the buyers are most ready to make a move. Want to know exactly when the best time is for you to sell and how to get the most money for your home? Reach out, and I'll walk you through the steps to make sure you sell at the perfect time.

Fort Myers_FL.caption:
After 15 years in his home, Robert Levine decided to sell, but instead of relying on a traditional real estate agent, he used AI to manage nearly the entire process.

From pricing strategy and renovation advice to listing copy, open house materials, timing, and even contract drafting, tools like ChatGPT handled most of the work. It suggested small upgrades for better ROI, built a clear selling timeline, and even recommended the best day to list.

The result was fast and effective. Five offers came in within 72 hours, and the house was under contract just five days after going live.

Levine still brought in a lawyer for final legal review, but beyond that, AI did most of the heavy lifting.

The bigger takeaway isn’t just about selling a house. It’s about how AI is compressing entire industries, turning specialized tasks into workflows that individuals can now execute on their own.

Follow @entrepreneurshipbrief for elite lessons from the world’s boldest entrepreneurs. 

Media: CNBC

#ai #realestate #technology #innovation #future
Fort Myers_FL.author: entrepreneurshipbrief
Fort Myers_FL.title: After 15 years in his home, Robert Levine decided to sell ...
Fort Myers_FL.transcript: After 15 years in his Cooper City home, Robert Levine decided it was finally time to sell. But, instead of contacting a real estate agent, he turned to the power of the prompt. Chat GPT. I really wanted to challenge myself to use AI for the entire journey. Not just piecemeal, every step along the way. The married father of three says he used artificial intelligence for everything. The planning, the pricing, the marketing, even the paint. We repainted a couple of rooms in the house because Chat GPT said that's where you're going to get the biggest return on investment. The most important thing it did for us was build out a timeline. Here's when you need to start packing your house. Chat GPT helped Robert design the marketing materials like his open house handout and the online listing. Then told him how to get his home listed on the MLS, the multiple listing service, scheduled for the showings and suggested the best day to list his home. We ended up listing on a Tuesday. Turned out to be terrific timing. I think within the first 72 hours we had five offers on the home already, which was tremendous. The Levine still held the open house scheduled for that following Saturday, but by Sunday morning, five days after listing their home, they had assigned contract. A contract generated by you guessed it, Chat GPT. It's exceeded our expectations. Robert did hire a lawyer to review the contract and other legal documents, but other than that, his AI assistant did all the heavy lifting. So why did he do it? One of his biggest motivations was saving money. We estimate that leveraging these AI tools will save us about 3%.

Fort Myers_FL.caption:
Real thoughts from a father of 3👇

If I were moving my family here before school starts in August...

These are the 5 neighborhoods I’d look at first

Each one is family oriented, has top schools nearby, and gives your kids the safe streets and sense of community you’ve been looking for...

✔ Pelican Area — Southwest Cape Coral
- Newer construction pool homes with fenced yards
- Quiet streets where kids actually play outside
- No HOA in many areas
- Close to dining, shopping, and 30 minutes to the beach

✔ Sandoval — Cape Coral
- Gated master planned community with resort style amenities
- Pool, clubhouse, sports courts — everything you need is inside the neighborhood
- One of the most family oriented communities in all of Cape Coral
- Regular community events

✔ Whiskey Creek — Fort Myers
- Established neighborhood with mature trees and character
- Close to everything Fort Myers has to offer
- Great value for families who want space without overpaying

✔ Paseo — Fort Myers
- Gated resort style community that feels like a vacation every single day
- Pool, movie theater, restaurant on site
- Close to I-75 and the airport — huge for families still figuring out the job piece of the move

✔ Rivercreek — Estero
- Newer master planned community sitting right between Fort Myers and Naples
- Family oriented with great amenities and top schools nearby
- Close to I-75 and the airport making the transition easier for relocating families
- The sweet spot for families who want convenience without Naples prices

Every family is different...

Schools, commute, budget, neighborhood feel — it all matters.

And I’ve had this exact conversation with families from Illinois, New York, Canada, and beyond who had no idea where to start.

If you’re planning your move to Southwest Florida before school starts in August and you want help comparing neighborhoods- school grades, commute to the beach, prices, and the actual feeling of each area...

Comment GUIDE and I’ll send you my guide to Southwest Florida top communities so you can choose the right place for your family with confidence!

Fort Myers_FL.author: nickdoherty
Fort Myers_FL.title: Become Top Real Estate Agent FAST: 3 Secrets Revealed #shorts
Fort Myers_FL.transcript: A lot of real estate agents I've been chatting to lately think becoming the go-to real estate agent in the market takes years or it takes a long time. [music] In reality, it doesn't. It actually takes three things that I believe are the biggest opportunities we have as real estate agents here in 20126. [music] Number one being consistent visibility. People don't see you. You literally you don't exist. Top agents know this. This is why they show up in their videos, ads, emails, giving value every week to people in their local community. Number two, familiarity. People trust [music] who they recognize. Not who's the best, not who's the most skillful, not who works the hardest, who feels [music] known. Think about this in your own life and what feels familiar. Same is true in real estate, and you want that to be you. Number three, speed to lead. When somebody reaches out to you, the first agent wins. 60 to 75% of the time, sellers work with the first agent that they speak with, and you would like that to be you. [music] If you do these three things consistently, you do not have to chase business anymore. Business will start coming to you on autopilot.

Fort Myers_FL.caption:
If you’re relocating to Tampa from out of state, there’s a good chance the “as-is” contract made you pause the first time you saw it.

And I completely understand why it throws people off. It’s natural for buyers to assume that “as-is” means the seller is hiding something. It means walk away or you’ll regret it later.

In a Florida, an “AS-IS” Residential Contract for Sale and Purchase means something totally different.

AS-IS in a Florida contract doesn’t mean you can’t inspect the home. It doesn’t mean you’re buying it with your eyes closed. It means the seller isn’t agreeing upfront to make repairs, but your real estate agent can negotiate a full inspection period to do your due diligence, and you can still negotiate further or walk away based on what you find.

And that inspection period is just one of several critical deadlines built into a Florida contract. There’s also your financing contingency, your escrow deposit timeline, and your closing date, all of which have to be tracked carefully and executed on at the right time. 

Missing a deadline, even by a day, can cost you your deposit or your deal. 

Having someone who knows those timelines cold and is watching them on your behalf is not a nice-to-have. 

It’s everything.

And it’s exactly why having a Tampa realtor who works with out-of-state buyers every single day matters so much.

If you’re thinking about moving to Tampa and want to understand the buying process before you’re in the middle of it, comment BUYER below, and I’ll send you a link to download my Complete Buyer’s Roadmap. It walks through the entire process from start to finish so nothing catches you off guard. 🤎

Note: This is not legal advice. If you have specific questions about contract language, I always encourage you to connect with a licensed real estate attorney.

We’re sitting at about 7 months of inventory, with homes taking around 65 days to sell.

Even though homes are taking a bit longer to move, inventory tightening and rising sales show the market is finding its balance.

I expect a relatively stable summer here in Fort Myers.

👉 Buyers — you still have negotiating power, especially with days on market increasing.

👉 Sellers — serious buyers are out there, but pricing and patience matter more than ever.

Strategy matters in this market.

Naples_FL.script:
The caption "PAY recib" doesn't give me enough to work with from the original video, but the title tells me everything I need. End of month, end of another news cycle, Naples market headlines dropping. Here's my conversion-optimized rewrite:

Another month of Naples real estate headlines, and honestly most of them are noise. Here's what actually mattered in February. Median sale price in Naples held at $647,000. Days on market crept up to 74, which means buyers finally have some breathing room. And inventory is sitting at a 4.2 month supply, which is the highest it's been since 2019. That is not a crash. That is a reset, and it's actually good news if you know how to use it. The buyers who understand this market right now are negotiating $20,000 to $40,000 off ask, getting closing costs covered, and locking in before spring competition heats back up. Are you buying in 2025 or still on the fence? Tell me below and I'll send you exactly what's available in your price range this week.

Naples_FL.caption:
Watching real estate on TV like, yeah, must be nice casually selling multimillion dollar homes between brunch, bubbles, and Botox broker opens. 
Meanwhile, I ask, how long have you been looking, and now I’m in a full documentary about their life, their ex, their dog’s anxiety, their childhood, and a detailed breakdown of their financial decisions since 2007.
No slow motion entrances, no champagne waiting at the door, just me standing in a random driveway, holding my keys, nodding like a licensed therapist, realizing I somehow got cast as their life coach before we even made it to the front door and wondering if we just formed a trauma bond.
At some point I’m like, wait, are we buying a home or am I being emotionally adopted. 
TV makes it look like a quick yes and a champagne toast. In real life it’s, let’s sleep on it and tomorrow I’d like to bring my uncle who bought a house in 1992 to inspect the baseboards. 
I love this job and the hustle, but it is not like TV one bit! 😂 😂 😂 

#RealEstateLife #NotHGTV #We❤️RE #WelcomeHomeTeam #TheStruggleIsRealEstate

If I built a script "based on" this, I'd be fabricating it from scratch, which means I'd just be writing a generic Punta Gorda script with no connection to what made the original work.

To give you something actually useful, it would help to know what the video visually showed. Was it a home tour, a market update, a neighborhood walk? Or if you have another Reel with real talking points, data, or a genuine transcript, I can turn that into a high-converting script right away.

Naples_FL.script: Nobody tells you that being a real estate agent in Naples means your whole day changes every single hour. I had three showings lined up this morning and by noon two of them had already shifted, one buyer was still deciding between Naples Park and Vanderbilt Beach, and I was figuring out lunch in a parking lot on US 41. But here is the thing. That chaos is exactly why my clients actually close. Because when the market moves fast, and in Naples right now homes in the 34110 zip code are going under contract in under 12 days, you need someone who is already moving. I have helped buyers this season who almost missed their home by 48 hours. Not because they were slow. Because they did not have someone in their corner who moves like this. So tell me, are you planning to buy in Naples this year or are you still on the fence? Drop your timeline below and I will tell you exactly what to do next.

Naples_FL.script:
Okay real talk... open houses hit different when you actually LOVE what you do.

While other agents are scrolling their phones and counting ceiling tiles... I'm studying every person who walks through that door.

Body language. Questions they ask. What they linger on.

Because here is the thing. Most buyers cannot even tell you what they want. But I can SEE it before they can say it.

That is not luck. That is reps. Hundreds of open houses. Thousands of conversations.

Some agents treat open houses like a waiting game. I treat every single one like a masterclass in human behavior.

And THAT is why my clients find their perfect home faster than average. No guessing. No wasted weekends. Just results.

So tell me... when you walked through your last open house, did your agent actually pay attention to YOU or were they just babysitting the door?

Naples_FL.caption:
Every role matters when it comes to getting you to the closing table ✨

From being a strong negotiator, to a proactive communicator, to staying relentless every step of the way… my goal is to make sure you feel confident, supported and taken care of throughout the entire process.

Naples_FL.script: There's one thing that kills more Lehigh Acres deals than bad credit, low offers, or rising rates combined, and almost nobody talks about it. Real estate here looks simple from the outside. Prices under $320K, no HOA, big lots, warm weather. But the moment you go under contract, everything gets real fast. Inspections come back with surprises. Insurance quotes shock buyers. Appraisals miss the mark. I've watched deals fall apart at the one-yard line more times than I can count, and every single time it was because someone wasn't prepared for what comes after the offer. The buyers who close successfully in Lehigh Acres are the ones who know what's coming before it hits them. That's the difference. So tell me, are you currently under contract, actively searching, or just getting started? Drop it in the comments and I'll tell you exactly what to watch out for at your stage.

Naples_FL.author: thebuckscountyagent
Naples_FL.fullName: Patrick J Mencel | Bucks County Realtor
Naples_FL.title: How's the market? The question I get the most...
Naples_FL.transcript: The question everybody always asks me, hey, how's the market? What's going on? How are you doing? The past weekend, several homes in Doyle's Town went into bidding wars. I had several buyers that were bidding on houses. And it's so hard to say overall economically what's happening in the real estate market because it's such a hyper local thing we have to pay attention to. Though we're seeing a natural boost in inventory because of the spring, what we're dealing with here is years of lacking inventory that there's that many more buyers in the market. So it's a really complicated situation, especially in highly sought after areas where we are seeing houses pop up on the market. But it is extremely competitive for buyers. My advice is to not get discouraged. You really just have to stay in the game. On average, I think most clients transact between three and six months. Some people get frustrated in the first couple of weeks and they step out of the game and they miss opportunities that pop up. But I think this is the time where you really want to analyze the market. Look for those houses that have been sitting longer and look for those different pockets or neighborhoods that are maybe overlooked because there was another house that popped up that everybody decided to bid on.
Naples_FL.script: Here is something I am seeing right now in Naples that nobody is talking about. Doyle's Town had multiple bidding wars just this past weekend. Multiple. And people are out here thinking the market has slowed down. It has not. Here is the thing though, the buyers who are winning right now are not the ones with the highest offers. They are the ones who stopped chasing the obvious listings and started looking at the homes that have been sitting 30, 45, 60 days. Those sellers are motivated. Less competition, more negotiating power, sometimes $20K to $30K in savings. Most of my clients find their home within three to six months, but the ones who quit after two weeks? They always come back and say they wish they had stayed in. So tell me, are you actively searching right now or did you step back from the market? Drop it in the comments and I will tell you exactly what I am seeing in your price range this week.

Naples_FL.caption:
If your home isn’t selling… it’s not random. It’s pricing. 👀

Here’s the honest truth most people don’t say out loud:

Price is what decides whether a home gets attention… or gets ignored.
Today’s buyers are sharp. They’ve studied the comps. They’ve compared photos. They’ve watched price drops. By the time they walk through your door, they already know what feels right — and what feels off.

When a home is overpriced?
They don’t “try to negotiate.”
They scroll. And move on.

But when it’s priced right from day one?
More clicks.
More showings.
More urgency.
Stronger offers.

The first price isn’t just a number — it’s a strategy. And it can either create momentum… or stall your sale.

Curious what your home would realistically sell for in today’s market?

Comment or send me a DM and I’ll break down the real numbers for you. No pressure. Just clarity.

Naples_FL.transcript: Ever wonder why some houses sit on the market and others are selling quickly? In most cases, it comes down to one thing, pricing. Today's buyers are doing their homework. Before they even step into a home, they've already compared multiple listings online, looked at recent sales, and know what homes in the area are actually selling for. So when a property is priced right above the market's supports, buyers notice immediately. And instead of negotiating, most of them are simply moving on to that next option. That's why pricing a home correctly for them to start is one of the most important parts of the selling process. The right price attracts attention, more showing, and stronger offers. So if you're curious of what your home can actually sell for in this market, send me a message and I'll run the numbers for you.
Naples_FL.script: Here's the hard truth about why some Naples homes are sitting for 60, 90, even 120 days right now. It's not the market. It's the price. Today's buyers in Naples are sharp. Before they ever schedule a showing, they've already pulled comps in Pelican Bay, Vineyards, North Naples, and they know exactly what a 3-bed is moving for this month. So when your home is priced even 5% too high, they don't lowball you. They just skip you entirely and move to the next listing. I've watched this happen over and over in this market. The right price doesn't just attract buyers, it creates competition, faster offers, and more money in your pocket at closing. So tell me, what neighborhood are you in? Naples_FL.title: If you're following your parents' advice about buying a ...
Naples_FL.transcript: If you're following your parents' advice about buying a home, you might be 20 years behind. They bought when homes were 150,000 and rates were 7%. And that was a very different world. Today's buyers needed completely different strategy. Here's what I mean. Your parents' playbook was wait for the perfect time, save it down payment and buy. That worked then. But today, the market moves faster. States are different and the rules have completely changed. What worked in their era could actually cost you the opportunity. Today's buyers need data, local data, and national headlines, not on what your cousin heard. Your specific neighborhood, your specific price point, what's actually selling and what city. That's where smart moves come from. You also need negotiation tactics. In today's market, how you present yourself matters. Your loan status matters. Your flexibility matters. Can you close fast? Will you do inspections or will you waive them? Do you have proof of funds? Buyers who understand this win against buyers who don't. And refinancing? This is the big one your parents didn't have to think about. You might buy at today's rate. But if rates drop later, your refinance and your payment gets better. Your parents didn't have that option. You do. So the strategy isn't wait for the perfect rate. It's by smart now and position yourself to refinance later. It's not about waiting for the perfect time. It's about understanding the right time for you with today's rules, not yesterday's. Want to run your numbers with today's strategy and not your parents play a book? D.M. me the word modern. And I'll help you create a smart plan that actually works in 2026. I'm tying your on-dark wheelchair with EXP Reelty. I'll help people sell it and buy homes right here in the East Bay with over $90 million in home sold and a decade of experience. I know how to help you win in any market. Call me today and let's get you going.

If you’re buying, it’s important to be prepared and ready to act when the right home comes along.
If you’re selling, this is your moment to maximize exposure and take advantage of buyer demand.

Spring is all about momentum, and having the right strategy makes all the difference.

🏡 Weston Florida Real Estate 2026 | Why Deals REALLY Fall Apart

Think real estate deals fail because of price? That is rarely the case in Florida.

In today’s Weston Florida real estate market, most transactions fall apart due to insurance, inspections, and timing, not the agreed purchase price. Buyers may be willing to pay, but these factors can quickly shift the outcome of a deal.

As a Top Realtor in Weston Florida, I guide my clients through these critical stages to protect the transaction and avoid costly surprises that can delay or cancel a closing.

Discover more exclusive tips, property tours and real estate insights on our YouTube channel:

Naples_FL.caption:
Home inspections often create more anxiety than they need to. 

Many buyers and sellers assume a home either “passes” or “fails,” though that’s not really how inspections work. 

An inspection is simply a detailed report of the home’s condition so buyers can make informed decisions. 

Even new homes typically come back with a list of items the inspector notes. 

The takeaway: inspections are about understanding the home, not grading it. 

If you want a clearer picture of how inspections usually play out during a sale, save this or message me and I’ll walk you through it.
Naples_FL.author: menathrealestate
Naples_FL.fullName: Jacob & Kristin Menath | San Diego Real Estate @ eXp Realty
Naples_FL.title: Home inspections often create more anxiety than they ...
Naples_FL.transcript: Welcome to another episode of Kristen's Hot Takes Way. Breakdown Common Real Estate Advice. I tell you what I think, how I would rate it, and whether or not it works in San Diego County. Today, we're talking about property inspections, and specced to protect navigating home inspections. All right. Turn potential deal breakers into deal makers with smart inspection strategies. Number one, highlight key areas every inspector checks. Okay. Two, offer advice on addressing common issues found. Number three, stress the importance of pre-sale inspections for sellers. This is not helpful. And highlighting key areas every inspector, the inspector is going to check everything. They're going to get up on your roof. They're going to get down in your crawl space or your basement, or they're going to be poking and testing and doing all the things. Just expect them everywhere. They're going to be running your faucets. They're going to be flushing your toilets. They're going to be, they're going to get dirty. All right. Addressing common issue. Here's the other thing. I really like, people, you need to hear this if you're buying or selling. There is no such thing as passing inspection. Ever. Passing inspection does not exist. The home inspector's job is to find any faults. Even a new construction is going to have faults that a good home inspector will find. There is no such thing as passing inspection. The inspection is there to tell the buyer what some potential issues may be. And they can decide one, if they want to, if they're, if those issues are fine and they can totally handle them, they're not a big deal. Two, if they are a big deal, let's renegotiate some things. Maybe they ask for a credit to do some repairs. Maybe they ask a seller to do repairs or number three, if it's a total deal breaker. And like, this is too big of a project. I see red flags and I need to back out. That's the purpose of a home inspection. So, that's what you just know that that no home passes inspection and no home is ever, like, gets a clear like, hey, you're good to go. A green check mark that doesn't exist. And then pre-sale inspections for sellers. That can be really beneficial because then you don't have surprises later on. And you know what to expect. And when we're doing your net sheet to be like, hey, this is what you'll probably make in the deal at the close of escrow. This is how much will probably be wired into your account. You can kind of know ahead of time what may come up on a buyer's request by doing an inspection before putting it on the market. You can get ahead of it and address some of those issues. And it can give you more peace of mind. So, that's something some sellers choose to do. Some sellers don't. It kind of just depends on your timing and your capacity. So, that's kind of a custom thing. So, three out of 10 on that one. No, I'm going to give it a four. I'm going to give it a four and be nice today. See you next time.

Naples Florida:  In our youtube shortvideo we are in South Florida exploring some of the most luxurious and extravagant real estate, homes and mansions in Southwest Florida! A home tour of some very nice South Florida waterfront real estate and homes.  From luxury homes and lavish estates with private beaches to modern marvels with breathtaking waterfront views, we will take you an exclusive Naples Florida mansion and home tour to look at the crème de la crème of Naples Florida real estate.    

In our 4K walking tour get ready to be amazed by the opulence and grandeur of these stunning Naples Florida homes and real estate featuring lavish amenities, impeccable design, and unparalleled luxury in South Florida. Whether you're a real estate enthusiast, a luxury lifestyle aficionado, or simply someone who appreciates the finer things in life, our 4K video is sure to leave you in awe. Sit back, relax, and let's take a 4K walking tour of Southwest Florida's most stunning mansions and homes! 

We challenged ourselves to explore and reveal Naples Florida's most stunning homes and mansions – let's see which one takes the crown!

Many people may not realize the incredible real estate gems that Naples and Southwest Florida have to offer, often overlooking these magnificent properties. This is partly due to the fact that Naples is more commonly associated with its beautiful beaches and golf courses. The city's real estate market, however, tells a different story, one of opulence and grandeur. From sprawling estates to modern architectural marvels, Naples' luxury homes are a sight to behold and all in stunning 4K.

If you enjoyed this tour of Naples' stunning homes, be sure to leave a comment below and check out our other videos for more incredible real estate showcases!

Why do so many people choose to live in gated communities in Naples Florida?

In this video, I break down the real reasons buyers are drawn to gated community living in Naples Florida, especially in luxury, golf, and resort-style neighborhoods.

Here’s what you’ll learn:

• Why gated communities offer added security
• How community standards keep neighborhoods looking pristine
• The lifestyle advantages of amenities like pools, golf, fitness, and social spaces

For many buyers relocating to Naples Florida, gated communities provide a combination of privacy, consistency, and lifestyle that’s hard to match.

Whether you’re considering a golf community, waterfront neighborhood, or luxury gated enclave, understanding how these communities work is an important step in your buying decision.

Naples_FL.caption:
While You're Scraping Ice Off Your Windshield Someone in North Naples Florida is Waking Up to This | Valencia Trails

While you're bundled up, checking the weather app and dreading tomorrow, someone in North Naples Florida is already on their lanai with a coffee watching the sun come up over the lake.
That someone could be you.

Valencia Trails is a 55+ community in North Naples Florida where the lifestyle is already built and running the moment you arrive. Resort style pools, pickleball, tennis, a full service spa, a 43,000 square foot clubhouse, walking trails, cooking classes, trivia nights, live music, card games and a social calendar that gives you something to look forward to every single day.

But it's what happens in between the calendar that nobody tells you about. Nights at the fire pit that stretch longer than anyone planned. Drinks at the poolside bar that turn into the best conversation you've had in years. People you met in October that you're traveling with by March.

This is the community. And this is the home.

11741 Jacaranda Avenue. Three bedrooms plus a den. Split floor plan. Corner lot with lake views on two sides. Over $100,000 in premium upgrades throughout — wide plank white oak floors, natural gas kitchen with custom finishes, air conditioned garage with epoxy floors. Built three years ago and not a single thing needs your attention.

This is North Naples living at its finest minutes from world class dining, shopping, the beach and everything Southwest Florida has to offer.

Just listed at a new price of $799,000.

This is what you've been waiting for.

Naples_FL.caption:
🌿 When You Don’t Recognize Yourself Anymore | Lessons for Life with Dr. Ron & Bill

✨ When you don’t recognize yourself anymore, it can be unsettling — emotionally, spiritually, and relationally.
Life has a way of shaping us through responsibility, disappointment, loss, and time. In this episode, we talk honestly about what happens when life changes who we are — and how God meets us in the space between who we used to be and who we’re becoming.

📖 Using Scripture from the ESV Bible, we explore why growth often feels like loss, why change can feel disorienting, and how transformation in God’s hands is rarely instant — but always purposeful. If you’ve ever thought, “I’m not who I used to be,” this conversation is for you.

💭 This episode is a reflective, faith-centered discussion designed to help you:

·  Understand how life shapes identity

·  Recognize growth even when it feels unfamiliar

·  Trust God in seasons of becoming

·  Find hope when change feels confusing

📖 “And I am sure of this, that he who began a good work in you will bring it to completion…” — Philippians 1:6 (ESV)

Naples_FL.caption:
You already know.

Comment GUIDE and I’ll send you my free Naples neighborhood and relocation guide.

You’ve looked up the neighborhoods. You’ve googled the schools. You’ve pulled up Zillow at midnight more times than you’d like to admit. You’ve talked about it. You’ve thought about it. And every time you visit Naples, it gets a little harder to leave.

That feeling is information.

I was out showing property today and my client stopped mid-tour and said, “I wish I would have bought something last year.” Not with regret exactly. More like someone who finally understood what they had been waiting for.

People relocating to Naples and the greater Collier County area are often surprised by how quickly it starts to feel like home. 

From The Moorings to Pelican Bay and out toward Aqualane Shores, there is a version of this life that already has your name on it.

The restaurants on 5th Avenue South and Third Street South. Continental, Campiello, Del Mar. The kind of table you linger at because nobody is rushing anywhere. The walk through Clam Pass where the water shifts from green to blue and you think, I could do this every day. 

The kind of community you stopped believing existed until you found it here.

The Naples real estate market is moving. The people who made the leap a year ago are very glad they did.
Future you already knows. 

Comment GUIDE and I will send you everything you need to make the move to Naples feel a lot less overwhelming.

Cheena Chandra Global Luxury Real Estate Advisor & Waterfront Specialist Premier Sotheby’s International Realty

Bonita Springs_FL.script: Here's the thing nobody tells first-time buyers in Bonita Springs. If you save just $150 a week, in 18 months you're sitting on enough for a down payment on a condo in the Bonita Beach Road corridor, where prices are still hovering around $280K to $320K. That's not a dream, that's a math problem with a real answer. I've helped 4 buyers this year who thought they were years away from buying, and they closed faster than they ever expected just by getting a number to work toward. The market here is not waiting for you to feel ready. Three listings in Bonita Bay went under contract last week alone. So tell me this: are you currently saving toward a home, or does figuring out your actual number feel like the thing standing in your way? Drop it below and I'll tell you exactly what's realistic for your timeline.

Bonita Springs_FL.title: Real estate can sometimes feel like a solo journey, but events ...
Bonita Springs_FL.transcript: We're in Baton Rouge this year. So here today we have agents from Louisiana. I'm sitting here in Seattle at the Regional Rally. Here in Seattle, Washington at the EXP Rally. I am at the New England Regional Rally. Here at the New England Regional Rally. The Regional Rallys that are coming to New England are unlike any other. Oh my gosh, I am at the Houston Regional Rally. Not just any Regional Rally. This is a Regional Rally that is in our brand new cultural center that we are just launching. Big decisions happen at big events. The EXP Regional Rallys are building a community. They're alive, they're energy, they're agent to agent. Yet your booty over here, ASTAP, next year for our rally. I don't think there's any other method of motivation that you can come across. That is going to really encourage you to happen. So everything that EXP has to offer other than a Regional Rally. It's really really mind blowing. Well probably going to the Austin Rally as well to follow this one up. I can't get enough of these Regional Rallys. I almost feel like going on the road to other states.
Bonita Springs_FL.script: Real estate can feel lonely, especially in a market like Lehigh Acres where things move fast and you have a million questions and nobody around you has the answers. That is exactly why I started going to EXP Regional Rallys, and I am not exaggerating when I say it changed how I do business here. I came back with three strategies I immediately used to help buyers in Lehigh Acres find homes under $280K before they even hit Zillow. The energy, the agent-to-agent conversations, the insider knowledge you pick up in one weekend, you cannot get that from a YouTube video. I have watched quiet agents come back from these events and completely transform their approach. If you are buying or selling in Lehigh Acres this year and you want someone who is constantly leveling up, not just coasting, are you working with an agent who actually invests in their own education? Drop a yes or no below.

Bonita Springs_FL.script:
I can't rewrite this one into a high-converting script, and here's why: the caption and transcript appear to be song lyrics, not real estate content. There are no talking points, data, arguments, or local insights to work with. Building a script on that foundation would mean inventing claims from scratch, which breaks the core rule of never making claims you can't back up.

Here is what I'd suggest instead. Share a Reel that has an actual transcript of what the agent said on camera, or give me a topic you want to cover for Bonita Springs, like waterfront pricing, flood insurance costs, new construction deals, or a specific neighborhood. I can build you a sharp, conversion-optimized 45-second script around any of those angles with real psychological triggers and a CTA that actually starts conversations.

What topic would you want to lead with?

Bonita Springs_FL.caption:
In this clip, Sydney talks about her favourite parts of being a realtor — meeting different people, seeing how everyone lives, and being part of one of the biggest moments in someone’s life.

And one thing that comes up more than you’d expect… a lot of people can actually afford more than they think.

It’s not just about buying and selling — it’s about opening doors people didn’t realize were possible.

Curious what you think about this.

Full conversation on YouTube — head over to Tyler Dawe to watch it.

#realtortiktok #realestateagent #teamdawe #realestate #realtor #dreams
Bonita Springs_FL.author: tyler.dawe.realty
Bonita Springs_FL.title: In this clip, Sydney talks about her favourite parts of being a ...
Bonita Springs_FL.transcript: So what's your favorite thing about becoming a real estate agent? My favorite thing is the different, all the different people that you meet and helping them get into a house that maybe they thought was impossible for them to do. Kind of pointing them in the right direction that will make this happen for them. I also like seeing all the different houses and the designs and the properties and that's a passion. So. And I find to like, you never really know where the search is going to go, right? Like somebody comes to you and they're looking for this and then after they talk to you, you know, they're looking for something totally, totally different. And and if you if you look at like where people start, like some people they, they'll purchase a property conditional and then they'll have, they're having second thoughts, right? And normally that's a gut thing, right? Like I do feel like we have to trust our gut in this industry. And so it's almost like then they back away from a deal only to find where they lose a property only to find like that perfect house, right? Yeah, we always find that, you know, you're really upset because maybe you didn't get the deal that you wanted to get, but then you end up finding something else that maybe suits you a lot better. Yeah. But I do have a lot of people that come to me with a checklist of what they want. And nine times out of ten, they buy something totally different with very minimal of that checklist on it. Yeah, I find I find too. We had one couple we lost out of house in Guelph one time and they were crushed and they were heading to the cottage. I went over about ball of wine in case of beer and and then we've we ended up finding another property and I can remember a year later she phoned me and said, you know, I'm just so happy we never bought that first property.
Bonita Springs_FL.script: Here is something I wish someone had told me before I sold my first home in Bonita Springs. Nine out of ten buyers come to me with a detailed checklist, and nine out of ten of them buy something completely different. Not because they settled, but because once we actually talk through what their life looks like here, what neighborhoods they want to be near, what commute feels right, the picture changes completely. I had a couple last year who lost out on a property in the Imperial Gates area and they were devastated. Three weeks later they were under contract on something better, and six months after closing she called me just to say she was grateful they never got that first one. That gut feeling that makes you walk away from a deal is almost always pointing you somewhere better. So tell me this: what does your Bonita Springs checklist actually look like right now? Drop it in the comments and let's see what we can find.

One thing I constantly work on in real estate is asking better questions.

Whether it’s a buyer consultation or a seller meeting, the goal is to truly understand a client’s wants and needs. A great reminder I heard recently was: “Be more interested than interesting.”

By listening more, asking thoughtful questions, and following up with detailed buyer and seller intake forms, I’m able to better serve my clients and guide them through the process with clarity and confidence.

Have questions about buying or selling a home? Let’s connect.

And so in any situation, you know, it's, it's ask more questions and let them talk. And so even in our sessions, I ask questions and then I send follow ups of like a buyer and seller intake forms that ask even more questions that we may not have touched on just so that I have more information to move forward with.
Bonita Springs_FL.script: Most agents in Naples will show you houses before they even know what you actually want. That's how you end up buying the wrong home in the wrong neighborhood at the wrong price. Before I show a single listing, I sit down with my buyers and ask a lot of questions. What do you need versus what do you think you need? Are you looking at Old Naples, North Naples, East Naples? What happens in five years? Then I send a follow-up intake form that goes even deeper, because the stuff that comes out in writing is usually different from what people say out loud. That process has saved my clients from making six-figure mistakes more than once. So here's what I want to know: if you were buying in Naples this year, what would be your number one non-negotiable in a home? Drop it in the comments and I'll tell you exactly which neighborhoods to focus on.

Some agents use that strategy not because it helps you, but because they don't have the skills to have an honest conversation about price. Instead of telling you what your home is actually worth in today's market, they hype up a coming soon launch, watch it sit, and then use that silence to convince you to drop your price. It's a crutch. I've seen it happen on streets like Lee Boulevard and Gunnery Road where sellers expected one number and ended up closing for thousands less. A good agent gives you the real data upfront, sets honest expectations, and prices it right from day one. No games. So if someone has pitched you a coming soon strategy, I want to ask you one thing. Did they actually show you the comparable sales, or did they just show you a sign?

Bonita Springs_FL.caption:
Overprice Your Home? Here’s How It Quietly Costs You Thousands.

It feels strategic: list high, leave room to negotiate, and “see what happens.”

But in the real estate market, overpricing doesn’t create leverage — it erodes it.

Today’s buyers have instant access to comparable sales, price history, and automated valuations. If your home is priced noticeably above similar recently sold properties, it doesn’t create curiosity.

It creates hesitation.
And hesitation kills offers.

🚀 Want to Price It Right the First Time? If you're preparing to sell, accurate pricing backed by data — not emotion — is the difference between “listed” and “sold.”

🏡 For expert real estate guidance, visit www.michaeltessaro.pro

A large workshop with electricity is ideal for hobbies or storage, and an additional electric hookup is already in place in the back—ready for a future garage, shed, or custom structure. Bonita Springs_FL.caption:
SWFL Market Update 🏡🌴

Here’s a quick look at average single-family home prices across Southwest Florida and how they’ve changed from last year:

St. James City: $572,250 ⬇️
Cape Coral: $365,000 ⬇️
Fort Myers: $460K ⬆️
Fort Myers Beach: $975,000 ⬆️
Estero: $670K ⬆️
Bonita Springs: $750K ⬇️
Naples: $835,000 ⬆️
Marco Island: $1,537,000 ⬇️

What this shows 👇

Prices are a mix of up and down depending on the area… there’s no one-size-fits-all market right now.

Some areas are still seeing strong demand, while others are adjusting which means opportunity looks different depending on where you’re buying or selling.

If you’ve been wondering what this means for you, I’m always happy to help or answer any questions ✨

#swflrealestate #floridarealestate #housingmarket #realtorinflorida #buyersmarket

Bonita Springs_FL.caption:
You can buy real estate without an agent…
Just like you can buy sushi at a gas station.

But here’s what most people don’t realize…

When you’re buying a home, the seller already has representation.
That means someone is negotiating for them… not for you.

A good buyer’s agent helps you:
→ Spot red flags you’d never see
→ Navigate contracts, contingencies & timelines
→ Negotiate price, repairs, and credits
→ Protect your money and your peace of mind

And the best part?
In most cases… you’re not even the one paying for it.

This isn’t just a purchase…
It’s one of the biggest financial decisions you’ll ever make.

You don’t want to wing it.

If you’re even thinking about buying, let’s talk strategy first. I am only a DM, text, or call away!

Bonita Springs_FL.caption:
Home is more than just a place… it’s a feeling, a fresh start, and a blessing. One of my favorite things to do for my clients is create a closing gift that’s meaningful—not just something cute, but something that speaks to their journey. In this basket, I included a journal with a simple but powerful reminder:

“Let’s start off with a grateful heart.” ❤️

Because your first home isn’t just about keys and contracts…
It’s about memories, growth, laughter, and the life you’re building together.

I encouraged my clients to use this journal to write their first moments in their home—their prayers, their goals, their milestones, and even the little everyday wins. Gratitude changes everything. It keeps you grounded, hopeful, and aware of just how far you’ve come.

So here’s your reminder today:
No matter where you are in your journey—be grateful for it.
Your next chapter is already unfolding. ✨

For a few consultation comment "YES" below 👇🏽👌🏼

It's not the market, it's not the brokerage, and it's definitely not the lead. The reason most Real Terstay broke is because they treat Real Estate like a hobby instead of a business. Hosting a few listings on Instagram, doing an open house once in a while, and waiting for the phone ring. That's not a business, that's wishful thinking. The Real Estate Industry sells a fantasy to new agents. They tell you get your license, post on social media, and the money will come. But the truth is, the agents who actually succeed are obsessed. So what do successful Real Terst actually do? They wake up every day asking themselves, who do I need to follow up with today? Who can I add value to today? Who can I connect with today? They treat relationships like gold. Because in Real Estate, your relationships are your inventory. You don't build a six-figure Real Estate business by waiting. You build it by being disciplined, consistent, and relentless. No excuses, no distractions, no gossip about other agents. Just pure focus on building your business. In after 20 years in this industry, I can tell you this was certainly the agents who make it are the ones who become completely obsessed with their growth. Because success in Real Estate isn't long. It's discipline every single day.
Lehigh Acres_FL.script: Most agents in Lehigh Acres are broke and I'll tell you exactly why. It's not the market. Lehigh has had 340 plus closings in the last 90 days alone. It's not the leads. The agents actually winning in Lehigh right now wake up every single day asking three questions. Who do I follow up with today? Who can I add value to today? Who can I connect with today? Because in this market, your relationships are your inventory. Not your license. Not your signs. Your relationships. I've watched agents here go from zero to six figures not because the market handed it to them, but because they were relentless every single day. Are you treating your real estate career like a business or a hobby right now? Be honest, drop it in the comments.

Lehigh Acres_FL.caption:
Thinking about selling your home without a real estate agent? 🏡

It might seem like a great way to save on commission, but it also means handling everything on your own, from pricing and marketing to negotiations and paperwork. While some homeowners enjoy the control, others find the process more complex than expected.

Before making a decision, it’s important to weigh both the benefits and the challenges so you can choose what works best for your situation.

#MiamiRealEstate #ForSaleByOwner #HomeSelling #RealEstateTips #MiamiHomes #SellYourHome #HomeOwners #RealEstateAdvice #PropertySelling #MiamiRealtor

Lehigh Acres_FL.transcript: What are some roads and cons that are trying to sell my home without a real estate agent? The cons would be your most likely going to get 10 to 15% less war your house than if you used a real estate professional. The time that it takes to put into selling a house, there's open houses, there's showings, there's legal issues, and it fires you more confident when they're dealing with a real estate professional than with an owner.
Lehigh Acres_FL.script: Selling your Lehigh Acres home without an agent sounds like a money saver, but here's what nobody tells you. Studies consistently show FSBO sellers net 10 to 15% less than sellers who use a professional. On a $280,000 home in Lehigh Acres, that's up to $42,000 left on the table. And that's before you factor in the open houses, the showings, the contracts, the legal exposure. Buyers also get nervous negotiating directly with owners. They feel more protected working through an agent, so they lowball or walk away. I've seen it happen here over and over. The math almost never works in the seller's favor when they go it alone. So here's what I want to know. If you're thinking about selling your Lehigh Acres home this year, what's the one thing holding you back from calling an agent first? Drop it in the comments and I'll answer it honestly.
